content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

November 17, 1989


Debbie Woods
Registrar of Voters,
City of Riverside
4175 Main Street
Riverside, CA 92501

Re: Combined Campaign Report
Our File No. I-89-620

Dear Ms. Woods:

     This is to confirm advice provided to your office on October
23, 1989, regarding the campaign disclosure provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ This letter constitutes writ-
ten advice under Section 84205.

     Section 84205 states:

     The Commission may by regulation or written advice
     permit candidates and committees to file campaign
     statements combining statements and reports
     required to be filed by this title.

     A special recall election has been called for the City of
Riverside. The election date is February 6, 1990. Because the
second pre-election statement due in connection with the recall
election (Section 84200.8) and the semi-annual statement due for
the last six months of 1989 (Section 84200) cover overlapping
periods, we believe it is appropriate for candidates being voted
on in that election, their controlled committees and committees
primarily formed to support or oppose candidates in that election
to combine those two reports.


1  Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Debbie Woods
File No. I-89-620
Page 2


Therefore, campaign disclosure statements should be filed on
the following schedule:

Type of Statement          Period Covered        Filing Deadline

1st Pre-Election          Jan. 1² - Dec. 23     December 28, 1989

2nd Pre-Election          Dec. 24 - Jan. 20     January 25, 1990
Combined with semi-annual
statement for the second
half of 1989

Semi-Annual               Jan. 21 - June 30     July 31, 1990

     If you have any questions regarding the above, please do not
hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5662.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Kathryn E. Donovan
                                        General Counsel

                                        [signature]

                                        By:  Lynda Cassady
                                             Political Reform Consultant

KED:LC:eab


___________________________
2  The period covered by any statement begins on the day after the
closing date of the last statement filed or January 1 if no
previous statement has been filed.

sections.conclusion: Therefore, campaign disclosure statements should be filed on the following schedule: 1st Pre-Election (Jan. 12 - Dec. 23, Filing Deadline December 28, 1989); 2nd Pre-Election (Dec. 24 - Jan. 20, January 25, 1990) Combined with semi-annual statement for the second half of 1989; Semi-Annual (Jan. 21 - June 30, July 31, 1990).
sections.question_synthetic: May candidates and committees in a special recall election combine their second pre-election statement with the semi-annual statement for the last six months of 1989, given that these reports cover overlapping periods?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC permits candidates and committees being voted on in the February 6, 1990 recall election to combine their second pre-election statement with the semi-annual statement for the last six months of 1989 because these reports cover overlapping periods. The combined report is due January 25, 1990.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a written advice letter confirming prior oral advice given on October 23, 1989. The document includes a detailed filing schedule table for the three required statements. No separate QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections are formatted as distinct sections, but the substantive question and conclusion are clearly present in the body text.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: May candidates and committees in a special recall election combine their second pre-election statement with the semi-annual statement for the last six months of 1989, given that these reports cover overlapping periods?

CONCLUSION: Therefore, campaign disclosure statements should be filed on the following schedule: 1st Pre-Election (Jan. 12 - Dec. 23, Filing Deadline December 28, 1989); 2nd Pre-Election (Dec. 24 - Jan. 20, January 25, 1990) Combined with semi-annual statement for the second half of 1989; Semi-Annual (Jan. 21 - June 30, July 31, 1990).

embedding.summary: The FPPC advises the Riverside City Registrar of Voters that candidates and committees in the special recall election scheduled for February 6, 1990 may combine their second pre-election campaign disclosure statement with the semi-annual statement for the last half of 1989 due to overlapping reporting periods.
